name: Widget & Live Activities
promptBlock:
Widgets and Live Activities: WidgetKit timeline providers, widget views, and Live Activity configurations require specific entry types conforming to TimelineEntry. The widget @main attribute must be on the WidgetBundle (not the app's @main). Place all widget UI code in the "WidgetExtension/" folder. Do NOT duplicate @main across the app and the widget extension. When using AppIntentConfiguration or AppIntentTimelineProvider, the App Intent type MUST be defined in a file inside WidgetExtension/ — the widget extension is a separate target and cannot see types from the main app.

ActivityAttributes MUST be in the main app target: Define the ActivityAttributes struct (e.g. FocusTimerAttributes) in a file OUTSIDE WidgetExtension/. Use "LiveActivity/<Name>Attributes.swift" (e.g. LiveActivity/FocusTimerAttributes.swift) or "Models/<Name>Attributes.swift". NEVER define it in WidgetExtension/ — the main app cannot see types defined there, causing "cannot find type 'FocusTimerAttributes' in scope" in ViewModels and Views. The app needs the type for Activity.request and activity.update; the widget extension must NOT define it, only reference it in ActivityConfiguration(for: YourAttributes.self). The build system compiles LiveActivity/ for both the app and the widget; WidgetExtension/ is widget-only.

SHARED TYPES AND DATA (CRITICAL): The widget extension is a separate compile target — it CANNOT see types defined only in the main app (e.g. a WidgetDataBridge struct in the main app will cause "cannot find type 'WidgetDataBridge' in scope" in the widget). For any type that BOTH the main app AND the widget extension need: define it in "SharedModels/<Name>.swift" (e.g. SharedModels/WidgetDataBridge.swift). The build system compiles SharedModels/ for both targets. For runtime data sharing, use App Groups: UserDefaults(suiteName: "group.com.bundleid") — write from the main app, read from the widget's TimelineProvider. Define the suite name constant in SharedModels/AppGroup.swift so both targets use the same string. NEVER assume @State, @AppStorage with default suite, or any in-memory app data is accessible from the widget; the widget runs in a separate process.

If the user asks for Live Activities, generate: (1) "WidgetExtension/WidgetBundle.swift" and "WidgetExtension/LiveActivityWidget.swift" with ActivityConfiguration(for: <YourAttributes>.self). (2) The ActivityAttributes struct in LiveActivity/<Name>Attributes.swift or Models/<Name>Attributes.swift — never in WidgetExtension/.
